questions.questionText: A boat is driven forward by a force of 800 N from its engine. Water resistance provides a force of 350 N. Calculate the resultant force on the boat.

steps.description: Identifies forces act in opposite directions

commonErrors: Adding the forces instead of subtracting
Not stating the direction of the resultant

questions.questionText: A van of mass 3000 kg accelerates uniformly from 5 m/s to 20 m/s in 10 seconds. Calculate the resultant force acting on the van.

commonErrors: Using final velocity instead of change in velocity for acceleration
Forgetting to divide by time to find acceleration

questions.questionText: A skydiver falls at a constant (terminal) velocity. Which statement about the forces is correct?
options: Weight is greater than air resistance
Air resistance is greater than weight
There are no forces acting on the skydiver

steps.description: Correct selection: weight equals air resistance

commonErrors: Thinking constant velocity means no forces act
Thinking weight must be greater because the skydiver is falling

questions.questionText: A 60 kg ice skater standing still pushes a 40 kg ice skater. After the push, the 40 kg skater moves at 3.0 m/s. Using the conservation of momentum, calculate the velocity of the 60 kg skater after the push.

steps.description: Momentum of 40 kg skater = 40 × 3.0 = 120 kg m/s

commonErrors: Forgetting that total momentum before is zero
Not using the negative sign to show opposite direction

questions.questionText: Explain the difference between mass and weight.

markScheme.answer: Mass is the amount of matter in an object, measured in kg, and does not change with location. Weight is the force of gravity on an object, measured in N, and depends on gravitational field strength.

steps.description: Mass is the amount of matter / measured in kg / does not change
Weight is a force due to gravity / measured in N
Weight changes with location / depends on gravitational field strength

commonErrors: Saying mass is how heavy something is
Not mentioning that weight is a force

questions.questionText: A cyclist uses a force of 250 N to travel 800 m. Calculate the work done by the cyclist.
